label: Pericyte_2_inflamm
evidence: Reynolds et al. (2021) identify two pericyte populations in adult skin: Pericyte_1 (non-inflammatory) and Pericyte_2 (inflammatory). Literature on human skin pericytes (Ganier et al. 2024) identifies RGS5+ and TAGLN+ pericyte subpopulations. The inflammatory pericyte subtype (Pericyte_2) corresponds to the TAGLN+/ACTA2-high population with upregulated expression of cytokines, chemokines, and adhesion molecules relative to non-inflammatory pericytes.
